subject: [PATCH v2 029/110] ext2: use PRIino format for i_ino

reviews.author: Jeff Linux-f2fs-devel
reviews.summary: The reviewer noted that the patch uses PRIino format for i_ino in various error messages, but did not check if the macro is defined before using it. The reviewer requested to add a check to ensure that PRIino is defined.

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer Jan Kara noted that the patch correctly converts ext2 i_ino format strings to use PRIino format macro, and also corrects signed format specifiers to unsigned as inode numbers are unsigned values.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza
-- 
Jan Kara <jack@suse.com>
SUSE Labs, CR

______________________________________________________
Linux MTD discussion mailing list
http://lists.infradead.org/mailman/listinfo/linux-mtd/

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch updates the ext2 file system to use the PRIino format for printing inode numbers, which will be widened in a future change via the kino_t type. The patch also corrects signed format specifiers to unsigned, as inode numbers are unsigned values. This change is made to prepare the ext2 file system for the upcoming widening of inode numbers.
